question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition against the cloud storage endpoint (S3/GCP) such that the 30-second `frequency` polling loop fires continuously with no circuit-breaker, causing unbounded thread-pool exhaustion and denial of service on the importer JVM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@DurationMin` constraint on `frequency` (only `@NotNull` is present) by injecting a near-zero duration via an environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_BALANCE_FREQUENCY=1ms`, causing the `@Scheduled` loop to spin at maximum rate during a network partition and starve all other importer threads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that forces `enabled=false` fallback logic in `AccountBalancesDownloader.shouldDownload()` to repeatedly query `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` on every 30-second tick, creating a database query storm that degrades the mirror node's database under partition conditions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause `StreamType.BALANCE` routing to silently stall while `StreamType.RECORD` continues, creating a permanent divergence between balance state and transaction state that is never detected or alarmed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a network path between the importer and the cloud bucket introduce intermittent packet drops timed to the 30-second `frequency` boundary, causing every download attempt to time out at exactly the `CommonDownloaderProperties.timeout` (30s), resulting in a 100% failure rate with no staleness alert triggered?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `enabled` defaults to `false` and the `shouldDownload()` fallback only downloads one initial file, so that a network partition occurring after the first successful download permanently halts all future balance ingestion with no error surfaced to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes repeated failed download retries while `persistBytes=false`, meaning partial or corrupt byte streams are silently discarded rather than flagged, allowing the attacker to suppress evidence of tampered balance files during the partition window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the importer to skip writing verified balance files to disk (`writeFiles=false` by default), so that after the partition heals there is no local audit trail of which balance files were processed, enabling undetected replay or gap attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition combined with `writeSignatures=false` to ensure that signature files fetched during the partition window are never persisted, making it impossible to forensically verify whether consensus was achieved on balance files ingested just before the partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a BGP hijack of the S3/GCP endpoint IP range, causing all balance download attempts to reach an attacker-controlled server that returns HTTP 200 with malformed balance data, and because `frequency` has no jitter or backoff, the importer hammers the attacker endpoint at a fixed 30-second cadence, amplifying the attack surface?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to exhaust the `CommonDownloaderProperties.maxConcurrency` (1000 HTTP connections) by causing all in-flight balance download connections to hang at the TCP level, blocking all subsequent download attempts including record stream downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DNS-based network partition (poisoning the cloud storage hostname) so that `frequency`-driven download attempts resolve to a black-hole IP, and because there is no minimum backoff enforced at the `BalanceDownloaderProperties` level, the scheduler retries at full 30-second speed indefinitely, causing log flooding and disk exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that occurs during the initial bootstrap phase (when `enabled=false` and no balance file exists in the database) to permanently prevent the `accountBalanceFileExists` flag from being set to `true`, causing the downloader to attempt one download per 30-second tick forever without ever succeeding?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `frequency` drives a `fixedDelay` scheduler (not `fixedRate`) so that during a network partition where each attempt blocks for the full 30-second `timeout`, the effective polling rate drops to 60 seconds, causing the balance lag to grow unboundedly and silently violating SLA without any alert?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause partial balance file downloads to be silently dropped (since `persistBytes=false` means raw bytes are not stored), then after partition recovery inject a crafted balance file at the same timestamp into the cloud bucket, which the importer accepts because it has no record of the original partial download?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `downloadRatio` (default 1.0, meaning all nodes) to result in all node download slots being occupied by hanging connections, preventing any new balance file download from starting even after the partition partially heals?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader's `frequency`-based scheduler to accumulate a backlog of missed invocations in the Spring task scheduler queue, and upon partition recovery, execute all missed invocations in rapid succession, causing a burst of concurrent downloads that overwhelms the database write path?
